issue_summary: Some tweaks to the Endpoints class
javadoc_before:
/** * An immutable {@link Collection} to represent the two (possibly equal, in the case of a self-loop) * endpoints of an edge in a graph. * *

The {@link Endpoints} of a directed edge are an ordered pair of nodes (source and target). * The {@link Endpoints} of an undirected edge are an unordered pair of nodes. The nodes can be * accessed through the {@link #iterator()}, and in the directed case, will iterate in the order * {@link #source()}, {@link #target()}. * * @author James Sexton * @since 20.0 */
javadoc_after:
/**
 * An immutable pair representing the two (possibly equal, in the case of a self-loop) endpoints
 * of an edge in a graph. The {@link Endpoints} of a directed edge are an ordered pair of nodes
 * (source and target). The {@link Endpoints} of an undirected edge are an unordered pair of nodes.
 *
 * @author James Sexton
 * @since 20.0
 */
